SEARCH
Package net.sourceforge.plantuml.packetdiag

Class PacketDiagram

java.lang.Object
net.sourceforge.plantuml.core.AbstractDiagram
net.sourceforge.plantuml.UgDiagram
net.sourceforge.plantuml.TitledDiagram
net.sourceforge.plantuml.packetdiag.PacketDiagram
All Implemented Interfaces:
Annotated, Diagram, WarningHandler, WithSprite
public class PacketDiagram extends TitledDiagram
PlantUML diagram implementation for the packetdiag syntax (bit/field-based packet layout).

This diagram collects packet items in declaration order, computes a row-based layout constrained by a configured frame width (number of bits per row), and renders the result as a TextBlock. Items that do not fit in the remaining space of a row are split across rows.

The diagram also renders a scale/bit indicator line above the grid. The scale can be customized through:

  • colWidth: number of bits per row (frame width),
  • scaleInterval: numbering interval for indicators (defaults to half of colWidth),
  • scaleDirection: left-to-right (LTR) or right-to-left (RTL),
  • sameHeight: optionally forces all blocks in a row to share the row maximum height.

      public static final int DEFAULT_COL_WIDTH
      Default number of bits per row (frame width) when no explicit width is provided.

  • Method Details

    • getTextBlock

      public TextBlock getTextBlock(int num, FileFormatOption fileFormatOption)
      Specified by:
      getTextBlock in class UgDiagram

    • getDescription

      public DiagramDescription getDescription()
      Returns the Diagram Description.
      Returns:
      the Diagram Description: "Packet Diagram"

    • checkFinalError

      public String checkFinalError()
      Specified by:
      checkFinalError in interface Diagram
      Overrides:
      checkFinalError in class AbstractDiagram

    • getColWidth

      public int getColWidth()
      Returns the number of bits rendered per row (the packet/frame width).
      Returns:
      the frame width in bits

    • setColWidth

      public void setColWidth(int colWidth)
      Sets the number of bits rendered per row (the packet/frame width).

      Values <= 0 are ignored.

      Parameters:
      colWidth - the frame width in bits

    • updateScaleInterval

      public void updateScaleInterval(int value)
      Overrides the indicator numbering interval (in bits).

      The provided value is clamped to [1..colWidth]. Once called with a valid value, the diagram no longer uses its default interval (which is colWidth / 2).

      Parameters:
      value - numbering interval in bits (must be > 0)

    • updateNodeHeight

      public void updateNodeHeight(int nodeHeight)
      Updates the height of a single bit cell (in pixels), used as the vertical scale for blocks.

      Negative values are treated as 0.

      Parameters:
      nodeHeight - bit height in pixels

    • setScaleDirection

      public void setScaleDirection(String dir)
      Public command-facing API to set the scale direction using a textual value.

      Accepted values are "LTR" and "RTL" (case-insensitive). Unknown or null values fall back to PacketDiagram.ScaleDirection.LTR.

      Parameters:
      dir - the direction string (e.g. "LTR" or "RTL")

    • setSameHeight

      public void setSameHeight(boolean sameHeight)
      When enabled, forces all blocks within the same row to share the maximum height found in that row.

      This is disabled by default to match the original blockdiag behavior.

      Parameters:
      sameHeight - true to enforce a uniform row height, false otherwise

    • addPacketItemRange

      public void addPacketItemRange(int start, int end, int height, String desc, int textRotation)
      Public command-facing API to add a packet item defined by an explicit bit range.
      Parameters:
      start - start bit index (inclusive)
      end - end bit index (inclusive)
      height - requested block height (in bit units, as interpreted by the renderer)
      desc - label/description to display inside the block (may be null)
      textRotation - text rotation to apply to the block label

    • getStyle

      public Style getStyle()
      Returns the merged style used to render this diagram.

      The style is computed lazily and cached for subsequent calls.

      Returns:
      the current diagram Style

    • getLastPacketEnd

      public Optional<Integer> getLastPacketEnd()
      Returns the last packet's end bit-position from the packet frame. If the system currently contains no packet, return empty.
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the last packet end position, or Optional.empty() if no items exist
